description:
You are playing the role of a backup-administrator who needs to archive a small SQLite
    database so that it can later be restored or audited.

    A database file already exists at:
        /home/user/databases/warehouse.db

    1. Verify that this file really contains a table named “products” with **exactly
       three** rows and the following columns in exactly this order:
           • id          (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY)  
           • name        (TEXT)  
           • stock       (INTEGER)

    2. Create an un-compressed SQL dump of the whole database and place it here:
        /home/user/backups/warehouse_backup.sql

       The dump **must** be created with the standard SQLite “.dump” command so that the
       very first line of the file is literally:
           PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF;
       and the last line is literally:
           COMMIT;

    3. Compress the dump using gzip so that the resulting file is **exactly**:
        /home/user/backups/warehouse_backup.sql.gz
       After this step, the plain “warehouse_backup.sql” file is still allowed to
       remain in the directory.

    4. Create a SHA-256 checksum of the compressed file and store it in:
        /home/user/backups/warehouse_backup.sha256  
       The checksum file must follow the canonical GNU coreutils format, i.e. **one**
       line of the form:  
           <64-character-hex-hash><two spaces>warehouse_backup.sql.gz

    5. Create a simple three-line log file at:
        /home/user/backups/backup_log.txt  
       Each line must look exactly like:
           [2023-03-15 12:00:00] <free-text message>

       Use these three fixed log lines (the timestamps *must not* be changed):

           [2023-03-15 12:00:00] Dump created: warehouse_backup.sql
           [2023-03-15 12:00:00] Compressed to: warehouse_backup.sql.gz
           [2023-03-15 12:00:00] SHA256 recorded: warehouse_backup.sha256

       The automated test will treat the entire content (including spaces and
       brackets) as literal text.

    Summary of artefacts that **must** exist when you finish:
        /home/user/backups/warehouse_backup.sql
        /home/user/backups/warehouse_backup.sql.gz
        /home/user/backups/warehouse_backup.sha256
        /home/user/backups/backup_log.txt

    No additional files should be created in /home/user/backups.
    Feel free to inspect the database before dumping it, but keep the solution to
    roughly 3–4 terminal commands.
truth:
Initial state
    -------------
    1. Directory /home/user/databases already exists with permissions 755.
    2. File /home/user/databases/warehouse.db is a valid SQLite3 database containing:
           CREATE TABLE products(
               id    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
               name  TEXT,
               stock INTEGER
           );
           INSERT INTO products(id,name,stock) VALUES
               (1,'wrench',42),
               (2,'hammer',17),
               (3,'screwdriver',58);

    Expected final state for verification
    -------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/backups/warehouse_backup.sql.gz
           • Is a valid gzip file.
           • When uncompressed, reproduces a standard SQLite “.dump” whose first
             line is exactly “PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF;” and whose last line is
             exactly “COMMIT;” (followed by a newline).
           • The dump contains the CREATE TABLE statement and the three INSERT
             statements listed above.

    2. /home/user/backups/warehouse_backup.sha256
           • Contains exactly one line, no trailing blank line.
           • Format: “<hash><two spaces>warehouse_backup.sql.gz”.
           • <hash> must be the correct SHA-256 of the gzip file created in step 1.

    3. /home/user/backups/backup_log.txt
           • Contains exactly three lines, each terminated by a single newline:
                 [2023-03-15 12:00:00] Dump created: warehouse_backup.sql
                 [2023-03-15 12:00:00] Compressed to: warehouse_backup.sql.gz
                 [2023-03-15 12:00:00] SHA256 recorded: warehouse_backup.sha256
           • No extra whitespace or blank lines.

    4. Directory /home/user/backups must exist with permissions 755 before the agent
       begins; if it does not exist, the agent is expected to create it.

    5. No additional files are permitted inside /home/user/backups; tests will fail
       if unexpected files are present.
name: task_000000_ac105346
